In this register the stories of the patriarch Joseph are depicted. title: Maestranze toscane, Pavimento marmoreo del Battistero
highlights:
Information** AuthorScripta ManeantCopyrightOpera di Santa Maria del Fiore
AuthorTuscan craftsmen
Date13th cent. CollocationBaptistery of Saint John
Specific locationFloor  ...    The floor of the Baptistery is laid in quadrangular “carpets” of green, white and red marble, with geometric motifs, zoomorphic and vegetal figures, made by Tuscan masters in the first decades of the 1200s. The work, known as*opus tassellatum*, reproduces motifs and patterns of Middle Eastern origin, probably derived from the fabrics imported to Florence by the Calimala guild. This guild, of producers and merchants in wool cloth, had supervised the  ...    Calimala*. For the Baptistery
